# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for edge computing security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.192.0/http/server.ts";

// --- Secure Plugin System for Deno Deploy ---

// Allowlist of permitted plugin modules (configured at deploy time)
const ALLOWED_PLUGINS: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(
  (Deno.env.get("ALLOWED_PLUGINS") || "").split(",").filter(Boolean)
);

// Allowed base paths for plugin resolution
const ALLOWED_PLUGIN_BASES: readonly string[] = [
  "https://esm.sh/",
  "https://deno.land/x/",
];

// Plugin interface that all modules must conform to
interface Plugin {
  name: string;
  version?: string;
  handle(request: Request, context: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<Response> | Response;
}

// Type guard for Plugin interface
function isPlugin(obj: unknown): obj is Plugin {
  if (typeof obj !== "object" || obj === null) return false;
  const candidate = obj as Record<string, unknown>;
  return (
    typeof candidate.name === "string" &&
    typeof candidate.handle === "function" &&
    (candidate.version === undefined || typeof candidate.version === "string")
  );
}

// Validate that a module path is safe to load
function validateModulePath(modulePath: string): { valid: boolean; reason?: string } {
  // Reject empty or whitespace-only paths
  if (!modulePath || !modulePath.trim()) {
    return { valid: false, reason: "Module path is empty" };
  }

  const trimmed = modulePath.trim();

  // Block relative paths, file:// URLs, and data: URLs to prevent local file access
  if (trimmed.startsWith(".") || trimmed.startsWith("/")) {
    return { valid: false, reason: "Relative and absolute local paths are not allowed" };
  }

  if (trimmed.startsWith("file://")) {
    return { valid: false, reason: "file:// protocol is not allowed" };
  }

  if (trimmed.startsWith("data:")) {
    return { valid: false, reason: "data: URLs are not allowed" };
  }

  // Block any path traversal attempts
  if (trimmed.includes("..") || trimmed.includes("\\")) {
    return { valid: false, reason: "Path traversal patterns are not allowed" };
  }

  // Ensure the module comes from an allowed base URL
  const isFromAllowedBase = ALLOWED_PLUGIN_BASES.some((base) =>
    trimmed.startsWith(base)
  );

  if (!isFromAllowedBase) {
    return {
      valid: false,
      reason: `Module must be from an allowed source: ${ALLOWED_PLUGIN_BASES.join(", ")}`,
    };
  }

  // Validate it's a proper URL
  try {
    const url = new URL(trimmed);
    // Double-check the origin after URL parsing (prevents tricks like https://esm.sh.evil.com/)
    const isOriginAllowed = ALLOWED_PLUGIN_BASES.some((base) => {
      const baseUrl = new URL(base);
      return url.origin === baseUrl.origin && url.pathname.startsWith(baseUrl.pathname);
    });
    if (!isOriginAllowed) {
      return { valid: false, reason: "Module origin does not match allowed bases after URL resolution" };
    }
  } catch {
    return { valid: false, reason: "Invalid URL format" };
  }

  // Check against the explicit allowlist if configured
  if (ALLOWED_PLUGINS.size > 0 && !ALLOWED_PLUGINS.has(trimmed)) {
    return {
      valid: false,
      reason: "Module is not in the allowed plugins list",
    };
  }

  return { valid: true };
}

// Cache for loaded plugins to avoid repeated imports
const pluginCache = new Map<string, Plugin>();

// Safely load a plugin module with validation
async function loadPlugin(modulePath: string): Promise<Plugin> {
  // Check cache first
  const cached = pluginCache.get(modulePath);
  if (cached) {
    return cached;
  }

  // Validate the module path
  const validation = validateModulePath(modulePath);
  if (!validation.valid) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid plugin path "${modulePath}": ${validation.reason}`);
  }

  // Dynamic import with timeout to prevent hanging
  const importPromise = import(modulePath);
  const timeoutPromise = new Promise<never>((_, reject) =>
    setTimeout(() => reject(new Error("Plugin load timeout (10s exceeded)")), 10000)
  );

  let module: Record<string, unknown>;
  try {
    module = await Promise.race([importPromise, timeoutPromise]) as Record<string, unknown>;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(
      `Failed to load plugin "${modulePath}": ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`
    );
  }

  // Validate the module exports conform to the Plugin interface
  const plugin = module.default ?? module;
  if (!isPlugin(plugin)) {
    throw new Error(
      `Plugin "${modulePath}" does not conform to the Plugin interface (must export { name: string, handle: Function })`
    );
  }

  // Cache the validated plugin
  pluginCache.set(modulePath, plugin);

  return plugin;
}

// Pre-load plugins specified in environment variables at startup
const ENV_PLUGINS = (Deno.env.get("PLUGINS") || "").split(",").filter(Boolean);
const startupPlugins = new Map<string, Plugin>();

async function initializeStartupPlugins(): Promise<void> {
  for (const pluginPath of ENV_PLUGINS) {
    try {
      const plugin = await loadPlugin(pluginPath.trim());
      startupPlugins.set(plugin.name, plugin);
      console.log(`[startup] Loaded plugin: ${plugin.name} from ${pluginPath}`);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error(
        `[startup] Failed to load plugin "${pluginPath}": ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`
      );
    }
  }
}

// Rate limiting for plugin loading (simple in-memory, per-IP)
const rateLimitMap = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();
const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 60_000; // 1 minute
const RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS = 10;

function checkRateLimit(clientIp: string): boolean {
  const now = Date.now();
  const entry = rateLimitMap.get(clientIp);

  if (!entry || now > entry.resetAt) {
    rateLimitMap.set(clientIp, { count: 1, resetAt: now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS });
    return true;
  }

  if (entry.count >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS) {
    return false;
  }

  entry.count++;
  return true;
}

// Clean up stale rate limit entries periodically
setInterval(() => {
  const now = Date.now();
  for (const [key, entry] of rateLimitMap) {
    if (now > entry.resetAt) {
      rateLimitMap.delete(key);
    }
  }
}, 60_000);

// Main request handler
async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const clientIp =
    request.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0]?.trim() ||
    request.headers.get("cf-connecting-ip") ||
    "unknown";

  // Security headers for all responses
  const securityHeaders = {
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
    "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
    "Cache-Control": "no-store",
  };

  try {
    // Health check endpoint
    if (url.pathname === "/health") {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({
          status: "ok",
          loadedPlugins: Array.from(startupPlugins.keys()),
          allowedBases: ALLOWED_PLUGIN_BASES,
          allowlistConfigured: ALLOWED_PLUGINS.size > 0,
        }),
        { status: 200, headers: securityHeaders }
      );
    }

    // List loaded plugins
    if (url.pathname === "/plugins") {
      const pluginList = Array.from(startupPlugins.entries()).map(([name, plugin]) => ({
        name,
        version: plugin.version || "unknown",
      }));
      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ plugins: pluginList }), {
        status: 200,
        headers: securityHeaders,
      });
    }

    // Load a plugin dynamically via query parameter (rate-limited)
    if (url.pathname === "/plugins/load" && request.method === "POST") {
      // Rate limit check
      if (!checkRateLimit(clientIp)) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Rate limit exceeded. Try again later." }),
          { status: 429, headers: securityHeaders }
        );
      }

      let body: Record<string, unknown>;
      try {
        body = await request.json() as Record<string, unknown>;
      } catch {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid JSON body" }),
          { status: 400, headers: securityHeaders }
        );
      }

      const modulePath = body.module;
      if (typeof modulePath !== "string") {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Missing or invalid 'module' field in request body" }),
          { status: 400, headers: securityHeaders }
        );
      }

      // Validate and load the plugin
      const plugin = await loadPlugin(modulePath);
      startupPlugins.set(plugin.name, plugin);

      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({
          success: true,
          plugin: { name: plugin.name, version: plugin.version || "unknown" },
        }),
        { status: 200, headers: securityHeaders }
      );
    }

    // Execute a loaded plugin by name
    if (url.pathname.startsWith("/plugins/execute/")) {
      const pluginName = decodeURIComponent(url.pathname.split("/plugins/execute/")[1] || "");

      if (!pluginName) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Plugin name is required" }),
          { status: 400, headers: securityHeaders }
        );
      }

      // Sanitize plugin name - only allow alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores
      if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/.test(pluginName)) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid plugin name format" }),
          { status: 400, headers: securityHeaders }
        );
      }

      const plugin = startupPlugins.get(pluginName);
      if (!plugin) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({
            error: `Plugin "${pluginName}" not found`,
            available: Array.from(startupPlugins.keys()),
          }),
          { status: 404, headers: securityHeaders }
        );
      }

      // Execute the plugin's handle method with a sandboxed context
      const context: Record<string, unknown> = {
        clientIp,
        timestamp: Date.now(),
        pluginName,
      };

      try {
        const response = await plugin.handle(request, context);
        return response;
      } catch (error) {
        console.error(
          `[plugin-error] Plugin "${pluginName}" threw: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`
        );
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Plugin execution failed" }),
          { status: 500, headers: securityHeaders }
        );
      }
    }

    // Default: show available routes
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        message: "Deno Deploy Plugin System",
        routes: {
          "GET /health": "Health check and system info",
          "GET /plugins": "List loaded plugins",
          "POST /plugins/load": "Load a new plugin (body: { module: string })",
          "ANY /plugins/execute/:name": "Execute a loaded plugin by name",
        },
      }),
      { status: 200, headers: securityHeaders }
    );
  } catch (error) {
    // Never leak internal error details to the client
    console.error(
      `[error] ${request.method} ${url.pathname}: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`
    );

    const isValidationError =
      error instanceof Error && error.message.startsWith("Invalid plugin path");

    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: isValidationError
          ? error.message
          : "Internal server error",
      }),
      {
        status: isValidationError ? 400 : 500,
        headers: securityHeaders,
      }
    );
  }
}

// Initialize startup plugins, then start serving
await initializeStartupPlugins();
console.log("[server] Starting Deno Deploy Plugin System...");
console.log(`[server] Allowed plugin bases: ${ALLOWED_PLUGIN_BASES.join(", ")}`);
console.log(`[server] Allowlist configured: ${ALLOWED_PLUGINS.size > 0} (${ALLOWED_PLUGINS.size} entries)`);
console.log(`[server] Startup plugins loaded: ${startupPlugins.size}`);

serve(handler, { port: 8000 });
